source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: The Career Jungler
archetype.receipt: Plays 5 roles but jungle is both the main and the best — 54.0% over 298 games, about 7.2 more wins per 100 than the weakest role. Lean into it.

entries.change: Move the carry games to jungle
Queue jungle instead of the usual mix
entries.receipt: 54.0% there vs 50.1% overall, 298 games of evidence
53.9% over 163 games on Khaimera vs 50.1% overall

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

pool.name: Khaimera
pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +33.6% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

pool.name: Argus
pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +13.9% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

pool.name: Greystone
pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime, physical survival and magical survival: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Krix: +18% on effective HP at minute 15.

pool.name: Murdock
pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +18.4% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

pool.name: Crunch
pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime and extended fights: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +14.1% on the burst combo at minute 15.

pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +17.3% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +14.7% on the burst combo at minute 15.

leanInto.name: Khaimera
leanInto.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +33.6% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

plan: Keep queueing jungle as primary — it is already Xeebs's usual queue AND best role at 54.0% over 298 games (overall is 50.1%).
Stop queueing carry: 41.8% over 184 games is costing real VP. When auto-filled there, play the safest comfort pick, not a learning pick.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Khaimera. Compared with the average player on the same hero, Xeebs wins about 3.3 more games per 100 on it.
Xeebs's champion pool is 10+ heroes wide and the top three are only 41% of the games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on the top three.
The math: Xeebs is at 349 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. The all-time peak is 692.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward the player's own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where the player wins, not why

insights.title: Vision is Xeebs's cheapest upgrade
insights.finding: 4.7 wards per game, 56% of the squad average. Wards cost nothing and they are being left in the shop.
insights.receipt: 4,021 placed + 244 cleared over 902 games; squad average 8.5/game

insights.title: Khaimera is the long-term relationship
insights.finding: 163 games and Xeebs genuinely out-pilots the field on Khaimera — about 3.3 more wins per 100 games than the average Khaimera player. The hours show.

insights.title: Mr_Meat31 brings out Xeebs's best
insights.finding: Xeebs's record alongside Mr_Meat31 is 54% over 240 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of the squad queue, make it this pair.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: A longest killing spree of 16 in real 5v5s (plus 2 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Xeebs has receipts.

coachReasoning.assessment: Xeebs's Aurora jungle win — 2/2/10 with 7 wards against a 4-ward norm — is the blueprint, while the losses came from fights taken without the tools ready: 11 deaths on Yin against a 7-death norm, and a Crunch game that soaked only 521 damage per minute against a 997 norm. The biggest lever is jungle on Khaimera, 65.6% over 163 games — the best number on the sheet.
plan: On Khaimera, take Demiurge (+33.6% on 20-second fights at minute 15) and pick fights around the minute-12 Viper spike — commit to long, grinding skirmishes where the sustain does the work, instead of jumping in and leaving.

On Crunch, enter fights only with the full combo ready: the 0/4/0 loss absorbed 521 damage per minute against a 997 norm, the sign of engaging without abilities up — the 56.6% record over 53 games was built on trading with everything loaded.

Ward like the Aurora game every time: 7 wards there against a 4-ward norm turned into a clean 2/2/10, and the season average of 4.7 wards per game is only 56% of the squad's — vision is the cheapest upgrade on the roster.
Shelve Yin for ranked after the 6/11/6 game (11 deaths against a 7-death norm) — run Khaimera or Greystone (54.8% over 93 games) in the jungle instead.
Treat carry as fill-only: 41.8% over 184 games is the biggest leak, and moving those games to jungle is priced at +7.2 wins per 100.

insights.title: Khaimera beats the field
insights.finding: About 3.3 more wins per 100 games than the average Khaimera player, over 163 games — the hours genuinely show.

insights.title: Jungle is home
insights.finding: 57.4% over 298 games against a 50.1% overall record — the main role and the best role are the same one.

insights.title: Wards are being left in the shop
insights.finding: 4.7 wards per game is 56% of the squad average — the Aurora win showed what happens when that number climbs for a night.

insights.title: The duo call
insights.finding: Alongside Mr_Meat31 the record is 54% over 240 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack.

coachReasoningSource: in-session pred-scout-coach agent, grounded in the report + the player’s recent reviewed games; every number ground-checked, failing lines dropped
